intake.risk_posture: Behavior-preserving broad continuation; do not treat size alone as a blocker.
intake.harness_policy: Use existing validation; record learning/lint/harness candidates, but do not edit harness or AGENTS.md without explicit later approval.
allowed_areas: domain collection scans only when directly feeding database/detail behavior
protected_areas: collection feature work except direct type/test fallout
dependency and lockfile changes without review
validation_commands: targeted vitest run --run commands per slice
intake.stop_condition: Concrete product ambiguity, unsafe scope expansion, dependency/harness decision, repeated verification failure, or a blocker that cannot be characterized safely inside allowed scope.

focus_areas.name: src/domain database/detail/reference layers

focus_areas.next: Implemented C1, C2, and C3; queued candidates C7 and C9.

focus_areas.next: Implemented C2 and C3; queued candidates C6 and C9.

focus_areas.name: popover/detail modal/tab controller flows
focus_areas.next: Active worker slice W1 covers C1; C7 queued behind it.

focus_areas.name: direct database/detail collection scans
focus_areas.next: Active worker slice W1 covers modal-host scans; C6/C7 lower priority.

evidence: Awakener, wheel, and global layers repeat lookup/card-name construction and rebuild wheel reference entries.

evidence: Hook still owns root/nested opening, async hydration, global trail ownership events, navigation, and root props construction.

evidence: Child schemas keep descriptionArgs/cardKeywords as unknown before canonical adaptation.

evidence: Renderer branches across text, skill, reference, stat, mechanic, realm, scaling, description args, plural args, and overlay icon effects.

evidence: Prior ledger scout found the file small and direct; route/tab behavior is not a current target beyond detail/modal flows.
candidates.next: Terminal unless a concrete tab/search route defect appears.

tasks.title: Focused domain/database/detail audit
tasks.expected_output: Ranked candidates with evidence, concept blast radius, validation ideas, statuses, and maintenance candidates.

validation: refactor goal checker passed before reconciliation

tasks.title: Choose first concept-complete implementation slice
tasks.expected_output: Select one bounded implementation slice with allowed files, invariants, validation, and rollback.

receipt.decision: C1 selected as W1 because two scouts independently found repeated modal-host lookup scans, the slice is inside user scope, behavior is covered by existing tests, and name-fallback characterization can be added locally.
receipt.root_fix: Build one detail lookup and reuse one non-awakener route-record loader instead of patching individual find calls.

tasks.title: Simplify DbDetailModalHost detail lookup and route loading
tasks.expected_output: Characterize name fallback, replace repeated overlay detail scans with one lookup, collapse non-awakener route modal loading branches, run targeted tests, and record receipt.

receipt.slice: DbDetailModalHost detail lookup and route-loading simplification
what_got_simpler: Overlay detail resolution now uses one lookup object rather than repeated array/getter scans.
Wheel, posse, and covenant route modal loading/render logic now share one generic non-awakener route component.
behavior_preserved: awakener route canonical tab redirect remains separate
missing overlay records still pop without route navigation
receipt.current_complexity: Before: repeated find/getCovenants/getPosses scans in overlay route/callback helpers and three duplicated non-awakener route modal components.
receipt.expected_complexity_after: After: one lookup build per overlay modal data set, O(1) id/name lookup for overlay refs, and one non-awakener route modal loader.
receipt.root_fix_patch_decision: Root-fix slice selected because the repeated scan/copy-paste branch smell had one local concept boundary and existing tests.
receipt.dependency_review: Not needed; no package or lockfile changes.
receipt.harness_update_considered: No durable harness update needed; added local characterization test.
risks: Full suite not yet run for this slice.

tasks.title: Choose second rich/reference lookup slice
tasks.expected_output: Select the next bounded implementation slice after W1.

receipt.decision: C2 selected as W2 because it is a high-confidence, bounded rich/reference slice and uses the reference layer's existing overlayByName seam instead of introducing a new abstraction.
receipt.root_fix: Thread overlayByName into RichSegmentRenderer resolution while keeping accessibleOverlays fallback for direct callers.

tasks.title: Use reference-layer overlay lookup in rich segment rendering
tasks.expected_output: Pass overlayByName to rich segment rendering, preserve existing overlay fallback behavior, add focused characterization, and run targeted rich/reference tests.

receipt.slice: Use reference-layer overlay lookup in rich segment rendering
what_got_simpler: Rich segment mechanic/realm rendering can use the reference-layer overlayByName map instead of scanning the overlay list per segment.
Direct renderer callers still retain the legacy overlays-list fallback.
behavior_preserved: Mechanic and realm token interaction behavior remains the same for normal database reference layers.
No visual output or callback contracts changed.
receipt.current_complexity: Before: each mechanic/realm segment scanned overlays and aliases with a loop.
receipt.expected_complexity_after: After: database rich text rendering does O(1) normalized overlay lookup through referenceLayer.overlayByName, with list scan fallback only for direct callers without a lookup.
receipt.root_fix_patch_decision: Root-fix slice selected because the reference layer already owned the normalized overlay lookup seam.
receipt.dependency_review: Not needed; no package or lockfile changes.
receipt.harness_update_considered: No durable harness update needed; added local characterization test.
risks: Full suite not rerun after W2 yet; W1 commit already ran the full pre-commit suite.
followups_not_done: C4 public adapter owned record index

tasks.title: Choose public detail adapter owned-record index slice
tasks.expected_output: Select the next bounded domain/public-detail implementation slice.

receipt.decision: C4 selected as W3 because public-detail child slot/family selection is in the user's adapter/resolver scope, repeatedly scans the same owned records, and can be refactored inside one adapter boundary with existing public-detail characterization.
receipt.root_fix: Create an internal owned-record index and adapt cards/talents/enlightens from it instead of patching individual find calls.

tasks.title: Index owned public awakener records before adaptation
tasks.expected_output: Replace repeated owned skill/enlighten/talent slot/family scans with an internal index while preserving current optional/missing behavior and adapter output.

receipt.slice: Index owned public awakener records before adaptation
what_got_simpler: Owned public skill slots, enlighten slots, and talent families are indexed once before adaptation.
Cards, talents, and enlightens read from the index instead of repeating find scans.
behavior_preserved: Required missing slots throw the same error text.
Passive talent and extraTalents ordering remains based on the owned talent array.
receipt.current_complexity: Before: each card/enlighten slot and named talent lookup scanned the owned child arrays independently.
receipt.expected_complexity_after: After: one pass builds slot/family maps; adaptation uses direct lookups.
receipt.root_fix_patch_decision: Root-fix slice selected because the repeated scans shared one adapter-owned concept boundary.
receipt.dependency_review: Not needed; no package or lockfile changes.
receipt.harness_update_considered: No durable harness update needed; existing public adapter boundary tests cover the behavior.
risks: No bespoke missing-slot fixture was added because the helper remains internal and existing public-detail adapter tests exercise composed cards/talents/enlightens and optional records at the public boundary.
followups_not_done: C5 awakeners full resolver patched-record casts

tasks.title: Choose resolver patched-record typing slice
tasks.expected_output: Select the next bounded TypeScript implementation slice.

receipt.decision: C5 selected as W4 because awakeners-full-resolver rebuilds detail records from patched maps with repeated terminal casts, and existing resolver tests cover skill, derived-skill, and overlay upgrade behavior.
receipt.root_fix: Replace casted requireCardRecord call sites with typed helpers that validate the expected record shape/discriminant once.

tasks.title: Remove patched-card casts from awakeners full resolver
tasks.expected_output: Replace `as AwakenerSkillRecord` / `as DerivedSkillRecord` patched-record rebuild casts with typed helper functions, preserve current errors and resolver output, and run targeted tests.

receipt.slice: Remove patched-card casts from awakeners full resolver
what_got_simpler: Patched card map rebuild now uses typed require helpers instead of terminal casts.
Wrong-shape patched records fail at one local narrowing boundary with explicit resolver errors.
behavior_preserved: Missing patched card ids still throw the existing missing-card error text.
Valid skill, derived-skill, and overlay upgrade behavior is unchanged.
receipt.current_complexity: Before: rebuildRecordFromMaps used repeated `as AwakenerSkillRecord` and `as DerivedSkillRecord` casts after loading from a union map.
receipt.expected_complexity_after: After: helper functions narrow once and return typed records to the rebuild shape.
receipt.root_fix_patch_decision: Root-fix slice selected because repeated casts had one local trust boundary and strong resolver tests.
receipt.dependency_review: Not needed; no package or lockfile changes.
receipt.harness_update_considered: No durable harness update needed; existing resolver tests cover the public behavior.
risks: No bespoke wrong-shape fixture was added; the helpers are private and valid-data behavior is covered by resolver tests.

tasks.title: Choose rich text parse-context slice
tasks.expected_output: Select the next bounded rich-text parser implementation slice.

receipt.decision: C3 selected as W5 because rich text parsing rebuilds normalized card-name and parse-option lookup state on every parse, and the direct parser/database-rich-text tests are strong enough for a behavior-preserving context extraction.
receipt.root_fix: Introduce a reusable parse context while keeping the existing parseRichDescription API as a wrapper.

tasks.title: Reuse rich text parse context
tasks.expected_output: Extract/reuse a rich text parse context so database rendering can avoid rebuilding normalized card-name/options lookup on every parse while preserving parse precedence and current public parser API.

what_got_simpler: Rich text parsing can reuse a normalized card-name and parse-option context.
Database rich text rendering memoizes that parse context for the current reference layer and record while keeping text/description args parse-specific.
behavior_preserved: parseRichDescription remains the public wrapper API.
Parser precedence for card names, excluded skills, overlay mechanics, stats, realms, scaling, and description args is unchanged.
Keyword footer text and descriptionArgs still belong to each parse call rather than the reusable context.
receipt.current_complexity: Before: parseRichDescription rebuilt the normalized card-name lookup and normalized parse options on every parse.
receipt.expected_complexity_after: After: callers that parse repeatedly for a stable record/reference layer can build a RichTextParseContext once and reuse it.
receipt.root_fix_patch_decision: Root-fix slice selected because the repeated lookup construction shared one parser boundary and the existing wrapper API could stay stable.
receipt.dependency_review: Not needed; no package or lockfile changes.
receipt.harness_update_considered: No durable harness update needed; added a focused parser characterization test for context reuse with per-parse args.
risks: Full pre-commit suite is pending for this slice commit.
followups_not_done: C8 public V3 child schema unknown fields
